body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### Agent supports it

Aider exposes `/model <name>` as a first-class in-chat slash command that swaps the **Main Model** mid-session; the official command reference lists it as "Switch the Main Model to a new LLM" (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html). A subsequent turn then runs on the new model. This maps to the capability key `features.multi_model_orchestration` in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` (already `true`), which is the primitive the scenario exercises ("per-turn model selection вАФ agent can change models between turns").

### Daemon can observe it (daemon_capability: full)

The scenario's three user-observable signals are:
1. After turn 1, `ModelName = <model A>`.
2. After turn 2 (post-switch), `ModelName = <model B>`.
3. `TotalTokens` after turn 2 > after turn 1 (accumulates across the switch).

All three fall out of the aider parser and tailer as they exist today вАФ a trace exists in the watched Source AND the parser handles it:

- When aider applies a `/model` switch it writes a `> Main model: <name> вА¶` blockquote to `.aider.chat.history.md`. The parser's `modelRE` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go:66`) matches **both** `> Model:` and `> Main model:`, updates `p.model`, and emits a `Skip` event carrying the new `ModelName`. This is pinned by `TestParser_MainModel_AfterSlashCommand` (`parser_test.go:210`), whose own comment states the case exists precisely so "model_changed scenarios" don't "silently fail because post-switch turns keep the original model name on Contribution.Model."
- Every model call closes on a `> Tokens: вА¶` line, emitting an `assistant_message` that stamps the current `p.model` onto both `ModelName` and `Contribution.Model` (`closeModelCall`, parser.go:153). So turn 2's usage is attributed to model B.
- `TranscriptTailer.applyModelMetadata` (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer_metrics.go:105`) sets `t.metrics.ModelName` on **any** event with a non-empty `ModelName`, which flows to `session.ModelName` and the dashboard. There is no "first model wins" latch вАФ the latest non-empty model always overwrites. The merge guard at `session.go:653` only *retains* the old model when the new one is empty/`unknown`; a real model-B name is never blocked, so the inter-turn switch refreshes the displayed `ModelName`.
- Token snapshots/contributions accumulate cumulatively across turns regardless of model, so `TotalTokens` after turn 2 strictly exceeds turn 1.

Transport is `FilesUnderCWD{ .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser }` (`agent.go:45`) вАФ a real local transcript exists, so there is no transport gap of the kind that blocks cloud-only scenarios. The mid-session model change is representable as a distinct observable: the post-switch turn's `assistant_message` carries model B's `ModelName`, which promotes `session.ModelName` to B at the message level even though aider emits no discrete `model_switch` event (the `> Main model:` blockquote is `Skip=true` state-only; the switch rides on the next turn's metadata). The scenario asserts the *resulting* model name and token totals, not a discrete switch event, so the daemon represents every required signal вАФ daemon_capability: full.

### Driver can record it (driver_capability: ready)

The recipe's step types are `send`, `slash`, `wait_turn`, and `sleep` вАФ every one is implemented by `scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh` (the `send|slash)` case types text + Enter). Crucially, aider's `/model <name>` accepts an **inline argument**: `cmd_model` (aider `commands.py`, "Switch the Main Model to a new LLM") raises `SwitchCoder(main_model=вА¶)` and switches directly with **no arrow-key picker**, so the switch is sendable via the existing `slash` step вАФ there is no `keys` gap (unlike the claudecode/codex picker recipes for this same coverage_id, which need a model-picker driver primitive). No `wait_turn` follows the `/model` slash (it produces a `> Main model:` blockquote with no `> Tokens:` close, i.e. no assistant turn); a fixed inter-turn `sleep` covers the switch window. Every step type this recipe needs is supported вЖТ driver_capability: ready.

### Matrix drift

The coverage cell currently records `daemon_capability: unknown` / `driver_capability: ready` with the legacy note "architect/editor pair detected per turn but inter-turn switch may not refresh." That `unknown` is resolved upward: the code path above plus the dedicated regression test (`TestParser_MainModel_AfterSlashCommand`) guarantee the post-switch refresh, so `daemon_capability` should move from `unknown` to `full`. `driver_capability` already reads `ready` and is confirmed (inline `/model <name>`, no picker). The maintainer should update the coverage row's `daemon_capability` to `full` and refresh the stale note.

## Confidence

0.85: the `/model` command is documented by the vendor, and the daemon-capability claim is grounded directly in the adapter source plus a pinning unit test; the driver-capability claim is grounded in the inline-argument `/model` form and the driver's step grammar. Held below 0.9 because the verdict is confirmed at the parser/unit level rather than by an end-to-end recording of an actual `/model` swap (no recording exists for this cell yet вАФ that's Stage 4's job, deferred pending aider provider auth in-env).
caveats: Aider does not emit per-turn cost in a form the adapter tracks (capabilities `cost_tracking: false`); the model name and token counts refresh across the switch, but cumulative cost attribution per model is not asserted by this scenario and is not tracked.

The `> Weak model: вА¶` line aider may also print is intentionally NOT matched by `modelRE` (parser.go:65) вАФ only `Model:` / `Main model:` drive `ModelName`. This is correct for this scenario (the weak/summary model is not the turn's main model) but means a switch that only changed the weak model would be invisible.

There is no discrete model_switch event: the `> Main model:` blockquote is Skip-only (state update), and the new model becomes visible only on the next assistant_message's ModelName. The scenario asserts the resulting ModelName + token totals (not a switch event), so this is spec-compliant вАФ but a switch with no following turn would leave the new model unrepresented in metrics until the next call.

End-of-turn is always synthesized via the parser's ~1500ms IdleFlush clamped to the SessionDetector poll (no in-band turn-end marker in the markdown transcript), so the workingвЖТready edge trails file quiet by ~5s; the recipe's 12s inter-turn sleeps exist to let each turn settle before the next prompt re-enters working.

sources.note: official in-chat command reference: `/model` = "Switch the Main Model to a new LLM"; `/architect`, `/editor-model` also listed

modelRE (L66) matches `> Main model: вА¶` (emitted after a /model switch) identically to `> Model:`, updates p.model; closeModelCall (L153) stamps p.model onto each assistant_message ModelName + Contribution.Model вАФ daemon_capability: full grounds here
TestParser_MainModel_AfterSlashCommand (L210) pins that the post-/model-switch turn carries the new model вАФ exists so model_changed scenarios don't silently keep model A
applyModelMetadata (L105) sets t.metrics.ModelName on any event with a non-empty ModelName вАФ latest-wins, no first-model latch; flows to session.ModelName вЖТ dashboard
merge guard L653 only retains old ModelName when the new one is empty/unknown; a valid model-B name is never blocked, so the inter-turn switch refreshes
Source = FilesUnderCWD{ .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser } (L45) вАФ a real local transcript exists; no transport gap

implements send/slash/wait_turn/sleep; the `send|slash)` case types text + Enter вАФ every recipe step type is supported, and inline `/model <name>` needs no `keys` picker вЖТ driver_capability: ready
features.multi_model_orchestration: true (the primitive this scenario exercises); features.cost_tracking: false (basis for caveat 1)
